caliber.dp_lanes: DP split: rank r serves global rows [r*2, (r+1)*2) of the same sequences, full 64 heads, num_local_sequences=2; MoE collectives unchanged (in-package all_gather -> itp partial -> reduce_scatter)
caliber.hc_backend: eager (E0sf frame); fused-vs-fused DP graph parity is re-checked at full config by the E1F dp settle gate
caliber.rep_lane: full replication: 8 identical sequences on every rank (E1F B semantics), num_local_sequences=8

dp_graph_vs_eager.judgment: bitwise per step (E0sf part-b class, DP shapes)

dp_vs_rep.judgment: (g3) diagnostic trajectory: per-layer bf16 MoE reduction-order noise compounds over 6 layers x 132 KV-feedback steps (near-tie router flips), same class as the replicated caliber's own cross-lane divergence; soft gate rms_rel <= 0.75 and finite

rep_cross_lane_baseline.note: replicated rank r vs rank 0 on identical sequences (zero on rank 0 by definition)

isolated.judgment: aligned-input per-layer isolation at position 8192: bitwise reported per sub-stage; every sub-stage bounded by the fp reduction-order limits in ISOLATED_LIMITS (kernel selection differs with the M dimension between the two calibers; the residue class is fp LSB, not semantic)
